frame_label: Other Man's Ownership > Woman's Own Consent
generated_comment: Rahul: I was talking to this girl at a pub and she kept saying she's not interested, but honestly these days people say that casually also. Once I found out she has a boyfriend, I immediately backed off yaar — I’m not that type, I respect that. If she was single, then at least there was a fair chance to try properly.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because he treats the woman's direct refusal as negotiable but accepts her unavailability only when another man's claim is established. It encodes the surface manifestation of 'backing off only after learning she has a boyfriend' and framing that as respect.
original_frame: Gendered Division of Chores via Practicality
persona_text: You are Rahul. Age 29. Married for one year. A software engineer in Bengaluru. You and your wife Neha both work full time. Neha has been saying for three weeks that the division of household work is not equal Ñ she cooks, she cleans, she manages groceries, she does the laundry. You do the dishes sometimes and take out the trash. You are not a bad person and you do not think of yourself as traditional. But you are on a phone call with your college friend tonight after Neha brought it up again at dinner and you are telling your friend your side of the story and why you think the current arrangement actually makes sense given both your situations.
